item.title: About
item.description: I am a lawyer and an economist. As part of my PhD studies at the University of Warsaw, I am conducting research on the relationship between the instability of law and its communicability. I also work as a Data Scientist. My research interests include: legal language and legalese - legal linguistics, theory, practice and praxeology of legislation, machine learning and statistics, the use of these in the study of law, philosophy of law, language and epistemology, literary studies, looking at language from the perspective of legal theory (anormative linguistics).

item.title: Data Science
item.description: About I work as a Senior Data Scientist at Silent Eight. If you need to analyze a set of data in a statistically reliable way, want to predict how a phenomenon will develop in the future or just like pretty charts – write to miryskiewicz [at] gmail.com. Selected problems I solved using machine learning: assessing the comprehensibility of Polish legal acts, prediction of insurance fraud, prediction of transactional fraud, exploration of factors affecting the success of advertising campaigns.

item.title: Research
item.description: Education since 2020, PhD, Law, Doctoral School of Social Sciences, University of Warsaw 2017-2021, Bachelor, Quantitative Methods in Economics and Information Systems, Warsaw School of Economics 2015-2020, Master, Law, University of Warsaw, with honours (summa cum laude) Publications Working on that :) Conferences 9 Jul 2022, Why the law will never catch up with reality and why it should nevertheless try, II Congress of Young Science, University of Gdansk 24 Jun 2022, A linguistic picture of the legal world, National Scientific Conference “Language and Identity”, University of Warsaw 3 Jun 2022, The hidden face of juridification - the specter of delegitimization of the human rights system, National Scientific Conference “Towards Global Constitutionalism?
